key_findings: 5-HT 2A and m Glu2/3 receptors co-localize but do not physically associate.
Activation of both receptors nullifies their inhibitory activities on glutamate exocytosis.
5-HT receptor antagonists amplify the effects of m Glu2/3 receptor agonists.
mechanisms: Inhibition of glutamate exocytosis by m Glu2/3 receptors
Functional interaction between 5-HT 2A and m Glu2/3 receptors
methods: Isolation of synaptosomes from rat prefrontal cortex
Pharmacological manipulation using receptor agonists and antagonists
outcomes: Understanding of receptor interactions in glutamate regulation
summary: This study investigates the interaction between presynaptic 5-HT 2A and m Glu2/3 receptors in the prefrontal cortex and their role in modulating glutamate exocytosis, with implications for neuropsychiatric disorders such as schizophrenia.
title: Pre synaptic 5-HT 2A -m Glu2/3 Receptor – Receptor Cross talk in the Pre frontal Cortex: Meta modulation of Glutamate Exocytosis
